scenes.title: What If History Felt Real
scenes.narration: What if history didn't feel like a textbook? What if it felt like you were there — inside the battle, inside the moment, inside the decision that changed everything?
scenes.visual_prompt: Gods & Glory cinematic documentary. Ultra-dramatic close-up of a Greek warrior's eye through a battle helmet at Marathon. Fire reflecting. The weight of a civilization in one face. Black and gold. Vertical 9:16 format.

scenes.title: Welcome to Gods and Glory
scenes.narration: Gods and Glory. Real battles. Real stakes. Real history — told the way it actually felt to the people who lived it. Every episode, every detail, built for people who want the truth, not the textbook.
scenes.visual_prompt: Gods & Glory cinematic documentary. The channel logo emerging from darkness — gold letters on black. Behind it, a collage of epic battle moments: Agincourt arrows, Roman legions, Viking longships. Cinematic, vertical 9:16.

scenes.title: What's Coming
scenes.narration: Marathon. Waterloo. Pearl Harbor. Agincourt. Vienna. Hastings. The battles that built the world you live in — and the men and women who fought them when it was not yet history. It was just Tuesday, and they had to decide.
scenes.visual_prompt: Gods & Glory cinematic documentary. A rapid-fire montage of battle locations across history: the plain of Marathon, Napoleon's battlefield at Waterloo, the ridge at Hastings, the Mongol fleet in a typhoon. Each image dramatic, cinematic. Vertical 9:16.

scenes.title: Subscribe Now
scenes.narration: Subscribe to Gods and Glory. New episodes dropping now. History that actually hits — because it happened, and it mattered, and it still does.
scenes.visual_prompt: Gods & Glory cinematic documentary. The channel name in gold against black. A subscribe button glowing. Epic orchestral energy. The promise of the channel in one frame. Vertical 9:16.
